source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Federal
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Promise’s Sales team partners with government agencies and regulated companies such as utilities to modernize how they deliver essential services. Our platform helps these institutions eliminate inefficiency, improve outcomes, and create a better experience for the millions of people who rely on them.

As an Account Executive, you will drive new and expansion business across targeted agencies and enterprises. Each customer has unique procurement processes, and successful AEs at Promise see these as opportunities to demonstrate creativity and value. Instead of relying on generic enterprise playbooks, you will take a scrappy, strategic approach that aligns Promise’s solutions with each client’s mission and procurement context.

You’ll work closely with GTM leadership to shape outbound strategy, open new verticals, and build a repeatable motion as we expand nationwide. This is a hands-on role in a high-growth environment where your success directly influences how millions of people access benefits and essential services.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and execute targeted account plans for government agencies and utilities.

 - Navigate complex and slow procurement processes with patience and precision.

 - Close new and expansion deals, delivering meaningful value to clients.

 - Establish and grow relationships with senior stakeholders across public sector and regulated enterprises.

 - Partner with Business Development and external advisors to generate and accelerate opportunities.

 - Respond to and win strategic RFPs.

 - Deliver tailored demos and presentations that align Promise’s products with each client’s needs.

 - Partner with GTM leadership to design outbound strategies, define personas, and refine messaging for new verticals.

 - Create structured prospecting workflows and continuously improve them based on performance data.

 - Collaborate with Marketing and GTM Ops to improve lead quality, CRM accuracy, and pipeline performance.

 - Deliver accurate forecasts and pipeline updates to GTM leadership.

 - Share prospect feedback to inform the product roadmap and influence new features.

 - Travel up to 50% to meet with customers and prospects.




WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - 5+ years of experience selling software into public sector agencies, utilities, or similarly complex regulated industries.

 - Proven track record of building and closing new business in long-cycle enterprise sales environments.

 - Strong network and ability to leverage senior advisors to generate outbound opportunities.

 - Experience developing sales playbooks, writing prospecting copy, designing cadences, and coaching teammates.

 - Metrics-driven with proficiency using CRM and GTM tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalents).

 - Demonstrated ability to navigate multifaceted procurement processes, from RFPs to enterpr

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - AI
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.






ABOUT THE ROLE

At Promise, we’re transforming how people in financial difficulty connect with public services—using technology to make systems simpler, faster, and more human. As a Software Engineer - AI, you’ll design and deliver end-to-end generative AI and large language model (LLM) applications that directly power this mission.

You’ll build AI pipelines and infrastructure for prompt engineering, fine-tuning, document parsing, and RAG systems, and help bring new AI capabilities from early concept into real-world deployment. You’ll be developing AI products that will be deployed across a variety of complex customer use cases. This role requires comfort operating in ambiguity and strong technical judgment about what to build now versus what to scale over time. Your work will drive innovation, automation, and efficiency across our platform—helping government services reach people more effectively and with greater dignity.

This is a chance to be on the frontier of applied AI: shaping systems that not only push technical boundaries but also make a real-world impact at scale.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build with purpose: Design and develop AI-powered applications and services, focusing on generative AI and LLMs.

 - Create pipelines: Implement services for generative AI integrations, document parsing, model fine tuning, and RAG systems, ensuring they are reliable and production-ready.

 - Drive automation: Partner across teams to identify opportunities where AI reduces friction, increases efficiency, and solves real-world challenges.

 - Stay ahead: Track and apply advancements in the AI landscape, guiding strategy and adoption.

 - Shape strategy: Contribute to the vision, goals, and roadmap for AI/ML development at Promise as an individual contributor with meaningful ownership over technical decisions.




NICE TO HAVE

While not required, we’re especially excited about candidates who:

 - Have taken a product or major feature from 0→1.

 - Have experience building in early-stage or high-growth environments.

 - Have founded or co-founded something — even if it didn’t succeed.

 - Have scaled AI-powered systems beyond initial prototypes.

 - Bring strong product instincts alongside technical depth.




WHO THRIVES AT PROMISE


YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE IF:

 - You are energized by big, complex challenges and the opportunity to solve them.

 - You want your work to have a real, measurable impact on people’s lives.

 - You take ownership and run toward problems, not away from them.

 - You’re energized by building in environments where not everything is fully defined yet.

 - You value clear, candid, and constructive communication.


PROMISE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:

 - You prefer hierarchy and rigid structures. We operate with freedom and responsibility.

 - You are uncomfortable with change. We move fast, adapt often, and expec

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer - Backend
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.






ABOUT THE ROLE

At Promise, we’re building technology that helps families stay afloat—offering flexible, affordable payment solutions that replace financial stress with stability. As a Product Engineer, you’ll design and scale the systems that make this mission possible.

You’ll spend most of your time hands-on with code, working on microservices and distributed systems, while growing alongside teammates and shaping our engineering culture. This is a role for someone who loves to solve complex problems at scale, thrives in collaborative environments, and wants their work to have real-world impact.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build with impact: Architect and implement scalable backend services that power our payments and financial products.

 - Collaborate deeply: Work with infrastructure, product, and forward-deployed teams to deliver reliable end-to-end solutions.

 - Code forward: Write and ship clean, maintainable, production-ready code.

 - Keep systems strong: Monitor, debug, and optimize distributed systems to ensure reliability and high availability.

 - Level up the team: Mentor engineers and contribute to a culture of learning, ownership, and engineering excellence.

 


WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Experience: 3+ years developing production software with a focus on backend systems.

 - Systems expertise: Passion for building and debugging large-scale distributed systems.

 - Cloud fluency: Familiarity with AWS, GCP, or similar cloud platforms.

 - Mindset: Curious, autonomous, and accountable—with a drive to own problems end-to-end.

 - Bonus: Experience in fintech, payments, or government technology environments.




WHO THRIVES AT PROMISE


YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE IF:

 - You are energized by big, complex challenges and the opportunity to solve them.

 - You want your work to have a real, measurable impact on people’s lives.

 - You take ownership and run toward problems, not away from them.

 - You value clear, candid, and constructive communication.


PROMISE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:

 - You prefer hierarchy and rigid structures. We operate with freedom and responsibility.

 - You are uncomfortable with change. We move fast, adapt often, and expect agility.

 - You want a typical corporate culture. We are mission-driven, ambitious, and direct.

 - You believe efficiency means “doing less.” We believe efficiency means doing better.




HOW WE SUPPORT OUR PEOPLE

At Promise, we invest in our team’s well-being, growth, and sense of ownership.

 - Equity for All: All full-time employees receive stock options to share in our company’s success.

 - 100% Paid Health Coverage: We cover 100% of base medical, dental, and vision insurance plans for employees and their dependents.

 - Hybrid Work: We collaborate in the office at least four days a week to stay connected and aligned as a team.

 - Flexible Time Off: Self-manag

source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Deployment Strategist at Promise, you’ll be on the front lines of impact- owning outcomes, not tasks. You’ll work hand-in-hand with government and utility partners to deeply understand their operations, pain points, and goals—then translate those insights into real, scalable solutions that drive measurable results.

This is not a passive customer success role. You’ll build, problem-solve, and strategize to ensure every deployment delivers lasting value—to our customers, their constituents, and Promise’s bottom line. You’ll push projects from launch to long-term growth, always focused on outcomes that matter: adoption, efficiency, revenue impact, and customer success.

If you love digging into complexity, building systems that work in the real world, and turning customer needs into scalable impact—this is your arena.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build real solutions: Partner with Forward Deployed Engineers to design and deliver systems that integrate seamlessly into customer workflows and drive measurable impact.

 - Own outcomes: Take full accountability for the success of each customer—from implementation to renewal, expansion, and long-term value creation.

 - Understand problems deeply: Get into the weeds of customer operations, data, and challenges until you can articulate their business better than they can.

 - Drive value and growth: Translate understanding into action plans that boost adoption, revenue, and efficiency—ensuring Promise becomes an indispensable partner.

 - Turn insight into innovation: Surface what’s working (and what’s not) from the field to influence product direction and future investments.

 - Operate with intensity: Anticipate problems before they arise, tackle ambiguity head-on, and maintain relentless focus on execution and results.

 - Communicate with conviction: Build trusted relationships across all levels—from line staff to senior executives—by delivering highly effective, innovative solutions.




WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Experience: 5+ years in roles driving real outcomes—consulting, strategy, implementation, or customer success—ideally in SaaS, fintech, or govtech.

 - Builder’s mindset: You thrive in ambiguity, move fast, and care about building systems that actually work in the real world.

 - Customer obsession: You go beyond surface needs to understand the “why” behind every request and solve for long-term success.

 - Analytical horsepower: You’re fluent in data, financial models, and metrics that define success—and you use them to guide decision-making.

 - Eye for growth: You recognize opportunities for upsell, renewal, and expansion while strengthening customer trust and alignment.

 - Cross-functional collaboration: You know how to turn insights from the field into product innovation by working shoulder-to-shoulder with engineers, PMs, and designers.

source_jobs.title: Bilingual Customer Support Associate (Call Center)
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.


ABOUT THE ROLE

The Customer Support Associate at Promise is a vital team member, helping individuals manage utilities debt by providing clear, compassionate support. In this role, you will handle client inquiries via phone, email, and text, guiding clients through Promise's services and available financial relief programs. Every call is a real person on the other end, and every conversation is a chance to make their day a little easier. Your role is essential in helping clients regain financial stability.




ROLE IMPACT

 - Real impact, every day. You won't just take calls - you'll help people work through some of the hardest financial situations they've faced, day after day.

 - Skills that grow with you. You'll work cross-functionally with our engineers to improve how we serve clients, and help launch programs that reach thousands of people - building a skill set well beyond a typical support role.

 - The heart of Promise. Customer Support isn't an afterthought at Promise - it's central to everything we do. You’re the front line of our work, and your knowledge and experience is invaluable to the company.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Respond to client inquiries through phone, email, and text with clear communication, clarity, accuracy, and compassion.

 - Assist bilingual clients in English and Spanish to fully utilize Promise's services.

 - Escalate complex issues to the appropriate teams for swift resolution.

 - Guide clients through applications for relief programs, reviewing documentation to help them get approved.

 - Maintain detailed records of client interactions to ensure consistent, high-quality service.

 - Contribute to company-wide initiatives and program launches as they roll out.


WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Bilingual in English and Spanish to effectively support a diverse client base.

 - Genuine empathy: comfortable listening to people in difficult situations and helping them find solutions with patience and kindness.

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to stay organized while handling multiple inquiries and delivering high client satisfaction.

 - Dependability, organization, and a positive attitude, even in a fast-paced, ever-changing day.

 - Availability to work on-site full-time in our Fairfield, CA office.

 - Preferred: At least 1 year of experience in a call center or client service role.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Background in program evaluation and benefits eligibility




HOW WE SUPPORT OUR PEOPLE

At Promise, we invest in our team’s well-being, growth, and sense of ownership.

 - Equity for All: All full-time employees receive stock options to share in our company’s success.

 - 100% Paid Health Coverage: We cover 100% of base medical, dental, and vision insurance plans for employees and their dependents.

 - On-Site Role in Fairfield, CA: Enjoy a focused, supportive environment

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer - Frontend
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

At Promise, we’re building technology that helps families stay afloat—offering flexible, affordable payment solutions that replace financial stress with stability. As a Product Engineer with a frontend focus, you’ll directly shape the way people experience that mission.

You’ll design and build high-impact web applications that connect individuals facing financial hardship with essential public services. We’re still early enough that your architectural decisions will shape the foundation of our product — but far enough along that your work will immediately impact real users at scale.

This is a hands-on role for someone who cares deeply about product experience and engineering craft. You’ll move fluidly between frontend architecture and backend integrations, using modern tools like React, TypeScript, and GraphQL. If you love turning complex systems into elegant user experiences — and want your work to matter — this role is for you.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build with impact: Architect and deliver scalable, reusable frontend systems and components that power our payments and financial products.

 - Craft exceptional experiences: Collaborate closely with product and design to create intuitive, accessible, and high-performing web applications that guide users through complex payment and relief flows with clarity and empathy.

 - Bridge frontend and backend: Partner with backend engineers to define and evolve APIs and ensure data flows cleanly from distributed systems to user interfaces.

 - Architect for scale: Help shape our frontend architecture, state management patterns, orchestration layers, and shared component libraries as we grow.

 - Raise the bar: Write clean, maintainable, production-ready code. Identify and resolve performance issues, edge cases, and UX friction points to maintain a high quality bar.

 - Lead and mentor: Support and mentor engineers, contribute to thoughtful code reviews, and help cultivate a culture of ownership, curiosity, and engineering excellence.

 


WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Experience: 3+ years building production-grade software, with significant experience in modern frontend development.

 - Frontend expertise: Strong proficiency in React and TypeScript. Experience building complex user flows, managing application state, and integrating APIs at scale.

 - Product mindset: You care about usability, performance, accessibility, and clarity — not just shipping features.

 - Full-stack fluency: Comfortable working across the stack — contributing to backend services (Node, GraphQL, REST, etc.) when needed to unblock progress and improve end-to-end delivery.

 - Systems thinking: Ability to translate complex distributed systems into simple, reliable user experiences.

 - Cloud familiarity: Exposure to AWS, GCP, or similar cloud platforms.

 - Bonus: Experience in finte

Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

At Promise, we’re building technology that helps families stay afloat—offering flexible, affordable payment solutions that replace financial stress with stability. As a Product Engineer with a frontend focus, you’ll directly shape the way people experience that mission.

You’ll design and build high-impact web applications that connect individuals facing financial hardship with essential public services. We’re still early enough that your architectural decisions will shape the foundation of our product — but far enough along that your work will immediately impact real users at scale.

This is a hands-on role for someone who cares deeply about product experience and engineering craft. You’ll move fluidly between frontend architecture and backend integrations, using modern tools like React, TypeScript, and GraphQL. If you love turning complex systems into elegant user experiences — and want your work to matter — this role is for you.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build with impact: Architect and deliver scalable, reusable frontend systems and components that power our payments and financial products.

 - Craft exceptional experiences: Collaborate closely with product and design to create intuitive, accessible, and high-performing web applications that guide users through complex payment and relief flows with clarity and empathy.

 - Bridge frontend and backend: Partner with backend engineers to define and evolve APIs and ensure data flows cleanly from distributed systems to user interfaces.

 - Architect for scale: Help shape our frontend architecture, state management patterns, orchestration layers, and shared component libraries as we grow.

 - Raise the bar: Write clean, maintainable, production-ready code. Identify and resolve performance issues, edge cases, and UX friction points to maintain a high quality bar.

 - Lead and mentor: Support and mentor engineers, contribute to thoughtful code reviews, and help cultivate a culture of ownership, curiosity, and engineering excellence.

 


WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Experience: 3+ years building production-grade software, with significant experience in modern frontend development.

 - Frontend expertise: Strong proficiency in React and TypeScript. Experience building complex user flows, managing application state, and integrating APIs at scale.

 - Product mindset: You care about usability, performance, accessibility, and clarity — not just shipping features.

 - Full-stack fluency: Comfortable working across the stack — contributing to backend services (Node, GraphQL, REST, etc.) when needed to unblock progress and improve end-to-end delivery.

 - Systems thinking: Ability to translate complex distributed systems into simple, reliable user experiences.

 - Cloud familiarity: Exposure to AWS, GCP, or similar cloud platforms.

 - Bonus: Experience in finte

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - AI
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.






ABOUT THE ROLE

At Promise, we’re transforming how people in financial difficulty connect with public services—using technology to make systems simpler, faster, and more human. As a Software Engineer - AI, you’ll design and deliver end-to-end generative AI and large language model (LLM) applications that directly power this mission.

You’ll build AI pipelines and infrastructure for prompt engineering, fine-tuning, document parsing, and RAG systems, and help bring new AI capabilities from early concept into real-world deployment. You’ll be developing AI products that will be deployed across a variety of complex customer use cases. This role requires comfort operating in ambiguity and strong technical judgment about what to build now versus what to scale over time. Your work will drive innovation, automation, and efficiency across our platform—helping government services reach people more effectively and with greater dignity.

This is a chance to be on the frontier of applied AI: shaping systems that not only push technical boundaries but also make a real-world impact at scale.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build with purpose: Design and develop AI-powered applications and services, focusing on generative AI and LLMs.

 - Create pipelines: Implement services for generative AI integrations, document parsing, model fine tuning, and RAG systems, ensuring they are reliable and production-ready.

 - Drive automation: Partner across teams to identify opportunities where AI reduces friction, increases efficiency, and solves real-world challenges.

 - Stay ahead: Track and apply advancements in the AI landscape, guiding strategy and adoption.

 - Shape strategy: Contribute to the vision, goals, and roadmap for AI/ML development at Promise as an individual contributor with meaningful ownership over technical decisions.




NICE TO HAVE

While not required, we’re especially excited about candidates who:

 - Have taken a product or major feature from 0→1.

 - Have experience building in early-stage or high-growth environments.

 - Have founded or co-founded something — even if it didn’t succeed.

 - Have scaled AI-powered systems beyond initial prototypes.

 - Bring strong product instincts alongside technical depth.




WHO THRIVES AT PROMISE


YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE IF:

 - You are energized by big, complex challenges and the opportunity to solve them.

 - You want your work to have a real, measurable impact on people’s lives.

 - You take ownership and run toward problems, not away from them.

 - You’re energized by building in environments where not everything is fully defined yet.

 - You value clear, candid, and constructive communication.


PROMISE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:

 - You prefer hierarchy and rigid structures. We operate with freedom and responsibility.

 - You are uncomfortable with change. We move fast, adapt often, and expec

source_jobs.title: Software Engineering Lead
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Promise is looking for an Engineering Lead to lead a high-performing engineering team, scaling our platform to meet the growing demand from cities, states, and utilities across the country. This is a role for someone who thrives at the intersection of technology, leadership, and mission impact-setting direction, building systems that last, and ensuring our work reflects Promise’s dignity-first values.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead by doing: Roll your sleeves up and build alongside the team, know the ins and outs of the product.

 - Own the roadmap: Partner with Product and Design to prioritize the right features—balancing usability, scalability, and measurable real-world impact.

 - Build for scale & trust: Ensure the platform is secure, composable, and capable of supporting governments and utilities nationwide.

 - Elevate the resident experience: Deliver features that make it easier for families to stay on track-like flexible installments, smart reminders, and personalized plans.

 - Stay hands-on: Dive into technical details when needed, from debugging production issues to optimizing system performance.

 - Automate what matters: Leverage AI and automation to eliminate inefficiencies, reduce operational overhead, and free teams to focus on what counts.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Partner with Customer Success and Operations to ensure seamless deployments and reliable performance in the field.

 - Raise the bar: Define and enforce standards for engineering quality, system reliability, and collaborative excellence.

 


WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Technical leadership: 6+ years building and scaling production software, including at least 2 years leading teams or projects.

 - Roadmap ownership: A track record of setting technical direction, aligning stakeholders, and delivering against ambitious goals.

 - Systems expertise: Strong background in backend systems, system design, and secure architecture.

 - Technical curiosity: Follows improvements in AI and other emerging technologies, and finds opportunities to turn new technology into useful technology.

 - Integration mindset: Experience working with legacy or third-party systems (government, fintech, or regulated industries a plus).

 - Problem-solving skills: Ability to address complex challenges with simple, scalable solutions.

 - Adaptability: Comfort operating in a fast-paced, feedback-driven environment where priorities can shift quickly.

 - Mission-driven energy: Belief in Promise’s vision that better systems create more equitable outcomes.

 - Bonus points: Experience in payments, civic tech, or public infrastructure.

 


WHO THRIVES AT PROMISE


YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE IF:

 - You are energized by big, complex challenges and the opportunity to solve them.

 - You want your work to have a real, measurable impact on people’s lives

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Promise is seeking a Forward Deployed Software Engineer who’s driven by impact, excited by challenge, and motivated by Promise’s mission: helping low-income families catch up on past-due bills and access the relief they deserve.

This is not your typical engineering role. You’ll be on the front lines—working hand-in-hand with utilities and government partners, solving complex integration challenges, and making sure our technology actually delivers where it matters most: in people’s lives. You’ll bring strong technical fundamentals together with empathy, patience, and the kind of customer savvy that turns a deployment into a long-term partnership.




ROLE IMPACT

In Washington State, Promise distributed $100M of energy aid in just three months, reaching nearly 700,000 households.

In Virginia and Florida, we helped ensure millions of dollars in water assistance funds reached families who might have otherwise gone without.

Our AI-powered eligibility verification now cuts approval times for programs like SNAP and LIHEAP from weeks to hours.

As a Forward Deployed Software Engineer, you’ll be on the front lines making this kind of impact possible.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Be the bridge: Work directly with utilities and government agencies to integrate Promise’s payment and relief products into their existing systems.

 - Problem-solve in the wild: Dive into customer environments, untangling complexity and crafting creative technical solutions that actually work in the real world.

 - Level up the product: Improve Promise’s products -- you will have the first hand context of what works and doesn't, if you see something to build, build it!

 - Travel occasionally (up to 40% of the time): Join customers on-site to make sure things run smoothly and strengthen our relationships in person.




WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Engineering chops: 4+ years of experience building and deploying production software, with fluency in environments that require adaptability and fast learning.

 - Customer instincts: Experience in a customer-facing technical role where you balanced empathy with technical rigor.

 - Tools of the trade: We use JavaScript / TypeScript, ETL tools, SQL, React – you don’t need to be an expert on any of these! You do need to come with a willingness to learn.

 - Curiosity & grit: You don’t wait for someone to tell you the answer—you explore, experiment, and document so others can follow.

 - Collaboration: This is a cross-functional role where you will be working across engineering, design, product, delivery, and sales. FDEs work across team boundaries to get things done.




WHO THRIVES AT PROMISE


YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE IF:

 - You are energized by big, complex challenges and the opportunity to solve them.

 - You want your work to have a real, measurable impact on people’s lives.

 - You take o

source_jobs.title: Product Designer - AI
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Product Designer at Promise, you’ll take the lead on turning ambiguous, horizontal problems into beautiful, intuitive product experiences that scale across products and customers. You will be a strategic partner to Engineering, Product, and Deployment Strategists, shaping strategy and driving clarity from discovery through shipping. You'll set a high bar for UI/UX and interaction design, steward our design system, and help transform real-world operational complexity - including AI-powered workflows - into experiences that residents and agency teams love to use.

This is a role for a market–leader: you’ll design, prototype, validate with users, and raise the bar for the whole organization while owning outcomes.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Shape design strategy for key product investments. Bring structure to amorphous, cross-cutting problem areas; define product primitives and patterns others can build on.

 - Design end-to-end experiences. Create flows, interactions, and high-fidelity UIs that simplify complex, real‑world workflows for residents, caseworkers, and operational teams.

 - Prototype to learn. Use rapid prototyping to explore approaches, validate with customers and GTM partners, and derisk big bets before we build.

 - Design for AI. Shape how AI surfaces in our product - from income verification and eligibility automation to intelligent payment plans. Design experiences that make AI behavior legible, trustworthy, and recoverable when it's wrong.

 - Evolve our design systems. Maintain and extend accessible, scalable components; ensure quality and coherence across surfaces.

 - Partner across the business. Work shoulder‑to‑shoulder with PM/Forward Deployed Engineering to ship; collaborate with Sales to gather field insights, shape narratives, and enable adoption.

 - Raise the bar. Lead critiques, mentor designers, and strengthen our product decision-making through clear storytelling and evidence.

 - Close the loop. Instrument and learn from usage, support tickets, and qualitative research to iterate quickly post‑launch.




HOW YOU'LL BE MEASURED

 - You consistently bring clarity to ambiguity and drive cross‑team alignment on the right problems to solve.

 - Your work ships with high interaction and visual craft, measurably improving adoption, completion rates, and time‑to‑value.

 - Our design system and patterns are stronger, faster to build with, and more accessible (AA+).

 - Partners across Promise cite you as a strategic peer who improves products, experiences, and outcomes.

 - You multiply the team—mentoring others, evolving rituals, and upleveling quality across the product surface area.




WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - 8+ years designing complex, multi‑surface digital products (or equivalent impact), including substantial ownership at a senior IC level.

 - A portfolio that dem

source_jobs.description:

We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As an IT Specialist at Promise, you will combine your technical expertise with a strong service orientation to support our company's internal tools, devices, and access and ensure stability and continuity across our internal operations. This position calls for a professional who embodies the spirit of a servant leader, capable of white-glove support with calm-under-pressure empathy. You will act as a key enabler of your peers’ operational efficiency, requiring not just technical acumen but also a collaborative and supportive approach to IT operations within our diverse tech environment.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Act as a primary responder for helpdesk questions and requests, managing each situation from initial detection through to resolution, ensuring minimal disruption and maintaining transparency with all stakeholders.

 - Own the process of provisioning and distributing laptops and workstations to our employees, ensuring the proper deployment of our security tools. This includes working directly with the MDM and security tooling to ensure a seamless experience for end users while enabling high security standards.

 - Track requests through our ticketing system and maintain lightweight runbooks and documentation.

 - Own and grow the process for providing high-quality support in response to requests for devices, new software, and user-level configuration changes.

 - Continuously assess and improve our workflows around onboarding and offboarding, and implement them smoothly and efficiently as we grow our headcount.

 - Own the uptime and maintenance of office equipment, including meeting rooms, A/V hardware, network gear, and user desk setup.

 - Interact with employees in a way that projects our company values while keeping clarity around policies and expectations for maintaining a strong security posture.

 - Maintain an ongoing commitment to personal and professional growth, enhancing your technical skills in IT operations and systems administration, while also developing the skills necessary for effective corporate security.




WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - High-ownership mentality with a willingness to spot a problem, understand user needs, propose a solution, and implement fixes efficiently and collaboratively.

 - At least 2 years of experience in technical support roles, particularly those involving IT support, technical customer support, or internal helpdesk, with a proven track record of problem-solving and clean documentation.

 - Proficiency with macOS and an ability to troubleshoot common usage issues, system settings, and application support.

 - Demonstrated ability to understand identity and access management concepts, and role-based permissions.

 - Exceptional ability to communicate complex technical issues clearly and effectively to a variety of audiences, adjusting to their knowled

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Platform & Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Promise is looking for a Platform & Infrastructure Engineer to help shape how we deliver and run software — with automation at the center of everything you do.

You'll build the systems that let us ship faster, smarter, safer, and better. Working closely with our Product and Forward Deployed Engineers, you'll give them the foundation they need to solve real customer problems — so they can stay focused on exactly that.

The through line of this role: fewer humans in the loop for the things that don't need them. That means owning deployments, environment setup, security, on-call triage, policy checks, cost reviews, and compliance evidence — and systematically taking humans out of those flows wherever it makes sense.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and own our GCP infrastructure, Kubernetes clusters, and Terraform IaC

 - Own CI/CD across all services — end to end

 - Build internal platforms and tooling that help product engineers ship with less friction

 - Use AI to automate platform work that used to require a human

 - Respond to incidents, own the postmortem, and close the loop so they don't repeat

 - Partner with Engineering, IT, and Security to build the pipelines our products run on




WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - 5+ years in software or infrastructure, with meaningful time in a platform, infra, or SRE role

 - Kubernetes experience in production

 - Solid Terraform or equivalent IaC experience

 - A track record of building internal systems other teams depend on and trust

 - You've been on-call for things you built, and you have real opinions about what that taught you

 - You use AI tools in your own work and can speak to how and why




NICE TO HAVE

 - Google Cloud Platform (our primary cloud)

 - ArgoCD experience

 - TypeScript or Go

 - Fintech, Payments, or GovTech background

 - SOC 2, FedRAMP, or PCI experience




WHO THRIVES AT PROMISE


YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE IF:

 - You are energized by big, complex challenges and the opportunity to solve them.

 - You want your work to have a real, measurable impact on people’s lives.

 - You take ownership and run toward problems, not away from them.

 - You value clear, candid, and constructive communication.


PROMISE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:

 - You prefer hierarchy and rigid structures. We operate with freedom and responsibility.

 - You are uncomfortable with change. We move fast, adapt often, and expect agility.

 - You want a typical corporate culture. We are mission-driven, ambitious, and direct.

 - You believe efficiency means “doing less.” We believe efficiency means doing better.




HOW WE SUPPORT OUR PEOPLE

At Promise, we invest in our team’s well-being, growth, and sense of ownership.

 - Equity for All: All full-time employees receive stock options to share in our company’s success.

 - 100% Paid Health Coverage: We cover 100% of base m

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Public Sector
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on track—while helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our mission is to transform public systems so they work better for everyone, especially the most vulnerable.



Our team includes experts from companies like Palantir, Google, Stripe , and esteemed government leaders. We work hard and believe deeply in what we do. We're looking for excellent people to build innovative, resilient technology.



Backed by over $50 million in funding from top investors – such as Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, The General Partnership, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Bronze Investments – Promise has been recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022,” “Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024,” and Y Combinator’s #1 GovTech startup.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Promise’s Sales team partners with government agencies and regulated companies such as utilities to modernize how they deliver essential services. Our platform helps these institutions eliminate inefficiency, improve outcomes, and create a better experience for the millions of people who rely on them.

As an Account Executive, you will drive new and expansion business across targeted agencies and enterprises. Each customer has unique procurement processes, and successful AEs at Promise see these as opportunities to demonstrate creativity and value. Instead of relying on generic enterprise playbooks, you will take a scrappy, strategic approach that aligns Promise’s solutions with each client’s mission and procurement context.

You’ll work closely with GTM leadership to shape outbound strategy, open new verticals, and build a repeatable motion as we expand nationwide. This is a hands-on role in a high-growth environment where your success directly influences how millions of people access benefits and essential services.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and execute targeted account plans for government agencies and utilities.

 - Navigate complex and slow procurement processes with patience and precision.

 - Close new and expansion deals, delivering meaningful value to clients.

 - Establish and grow relationships with senior stakeholders across public sector and regulated enterprises.

 - Partner with Business Development and external advisors to generate and accelerate opportunities.

 - Respond to and win strategic RFPs.

 - Deliver tailored demos and presentations that align Promise’s products with each client’s needs.

 - Partner with GTM leadership to design outbound strategies, define personas, and refine messaging for new verticals.

 - Create structured prospecting workflows and continuously improve them based on performance data.

 - Collaborate with Marketing and GTM Ops to improve lead quality, CRM accuracy, and pipeline performance.

 - Deliver accurate forecasts and pipeline updates to GTM leadership.

 - Share prospect feedback to inform the product roadmap and influence new features.

 - Travel up to 50% to meet with customers and prospects.




WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - 5+ years of experience selling software into public sector agencies, utilities, or similarly complex regulated industries.

 - Proven track record of building and closing new business in long-cycle enterprise sales environments.

 - Strong network and ability to leverage senior advisors to generate outbound opportunities.

 - Experience developing sales playbooks, writing prospecting copy, designing cadences, and coaching teammates.

 - Metrics-driven with proficiency using CRM and GTM tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalents).

 - Demonstrated ability to navigate multifaceted procurement processes, from RFPs to enterpr

notes: High tech role ratio, frequent postings, and a strong engineering core suggest an engineering-heavy primary function.
